title: Far Off-Shore
content:
Look, the raft, a signal flying,
    Thin--a shred;
    None upon the lashed spars lying,
    Quick or dead.

    Cries the sea-fowl, hovering over,
    "Crew, the crew?"
    And the billow, reckless, rover,
    Sweeps anew!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Herman Melville, titled "Far Off-Shore", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
